title: ?server= port validated lazily, not at from_uri() construction
description: from_uri() accepts 'secretx:wolfhsm:key?server=host:40000' (stores the address string). The InvalidUri for port>32767 only surfaces when get()/put() is called, not at construction time. CLAUDE.md says: 'from_uri constructs the backend object and validates URI syntax only — no network call, no file read.' make_transport is pure string parsing (no I/O), so calling it in from_uri() for a non-empty ?server= is within contract. Fix: call make_transport() on the server param in from_uri() when it is non-empty; return InvalidUri immediately if it fails. The WOLFHSM_SERVER env-var path still validates lazily (only known at first use), which is fine.
